frame_analyses.description: Setup view showing laparoscopic trainer with Penrose drain mounted on foam block, two marks visible on drain, suture material present
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean setup, marks clearly visible on drain

frame_analyses.description: Right needle driver grasping needle, left grasper holding suture tail
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle grasped at appropriate distance from tip, good initial positioning

frame_analyses.description: Needle being positioned for entry through first mark on drain
Needle passing through drain at second mark location
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean passage through tissue, good needle control

frame_analyses.description: Beginning knot formation, suture being wrapped around needle driver
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Starting surgeon's knot formation with right hand leading

frame_analyses.description: Completing first throw of surgeon's knot, double wrap visible
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Good double throw technique, appropriate tension maintained

frame_analyses.description: Beginning second throw with left hand leading the wrap
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper hand switching, left instrument now leading

frame_analyses.description: Completing final throw with right hand leading again
Cutting suture tails with scissors inside trainer box
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Both tails being cut appropriately short

penalties.description: Entry point appears 1-2mm medial to first mark

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double throw with appropriate tension
Proper hand switch executed, single throw completed
Final throw with proper hand alternation

confidence_rationale: Excellent visualization of knot tying sequence and hand switching clearly visible. Minor uncertainty in exact suture placement deviation measurements due to camera angle during needle entry.
cannot_determine: Exact millimeter deviation measurements at entry points due to single camera angle
Precise knot security assessment without tension testing
technique_summary: Excellent expert-level performance with rapid completion time and proper technique execution. Clean knot sequence with appropriate hand alternation.
strengths: Rapid completion time of 66 seconds (expert level)
Perfect hand switching sequence across all three throws
Smooth instrument handling and knot formation
Appropriate suture tail cutting inside trainer
improvement_suggestions: Focus on more precise needle entry at mark centers to minimize deviation penalties
Consider slight adjustment to final throw timing to maintain expert benchmark
